restore_note: This section's cases were accidentally deleted from TestRail (section tree intact, 0 cases live). Restored from the last-known content of ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json, converted to draft schema for a fresh /grill-section pass before republishing. No original Suite 6 source_case_ids were recorded when this was first migrated.

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that the forgot password page renders with all required fields and controls
cases.preconditions: User is on the Forgot Password page.

steps.content: Verify the Email address field is present.
steps.expected: Email address field should be visible. It carries no visual required marker (no asterisk, no `required`/`aria-required` attribute) and is a plain `type="text"` input (not `type="email"`, so no native browser format validation applies here either) — its mandatory nature is enforced only on submit.

steps.content: Verify the "Submit" button is present.
steps.expected: "Submit" button should be visible and interactive.

steps.content: Verify the "Back to Login" link is present.
steps.expected: "Back to Login" link should be visible and interactive.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json) — no content change.

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that the "Back to Login" link on the forgot password page navigates to the login page
cases.preconditions: User is on the Forgot Password page.

steps.content: Click "Back to Login".
steps.expected: Login page should be displayed.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json) — no content change. No run_type set in the original publish.

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that submitting a valid email on the forgot password page sends a password reset email
cases.preconditions: User is on the Forgot Password page.

steps.content: Enter a valid registered email address in the email field.
steps.expected: Email should be entered.

steps.content: Click "Submit".
steps.expected: The check-inbox confirmation page should be displayed.

steps.content: Verify that a "Password Reset Request" email is received at the registered email address.
steps.expected: Password reset email should be received.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json) — no content change. Steps 1-2 confirmed live: submitting a registered email posts to /api/v2/password/tokens (response never exposes the token — no dev/QA shortcut available) and the check-inbox page appears, quoting the submitted email address back. Step 3 (actual email receipt) is not automatable — no inbox access configured for the test accounts.

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that clicking the "Reset Password" button in the reset email opens the reset password page
cases.preconditions: User has submitted a valid email on the Forgot Password page and has received a "Password Reset Request" email.

steps.content: Open the "Password Reset Request" email in the inbox.
steps.expected: Email should be accessible and opened.

steps.content: Click the "Reset Password" button in the email.
steps.expected: The Reset Password page should be displayed at `/reset/password?token=<token>`.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json) — no content change. URL shape confirmed live via a real reset link provided out-of-band by the user (opening the actual email itself is still not automatable — no inbox access configured).

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that the reset password page renders with all required fields and controls
cases.preconditions: User has clicked "Reset Password" in the reset email and is on the Reset Password page.

steps.content: Verify the Email address field is present.
steps.expected: Email address field should be visible. It is NOT server-prefilled — the token-verification API (`GET /api/v2/password/verify-token?token=...`) returns only `{"status": true}`, no email; any value seen in the field on a real browser is Chrome's own autofill guessing from browser history, not app behavior. No visual required marker (no asterisk, no `required`/`aria-required` attribute) — enforced only on submit.

steps.content: Verify the New password field is present.
steps.expected: New password field should be visible. No show/hide eye-icon toggle exists on this field (unlike the Login page's password field). No visual required marker.

steps.content: Verify the Confirm password field is present.
steps.expected: Confirm password field should be visible. No show/hide toggle here either. No visual required marker.

steps.content: Verify the "Reset Password" button is present.
steps.expected: "Reset Password" button should be visible and interactive.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json) — no content change. Verified live via a real reset link provided out-of-band. Structural context added: above the fields, the page shows an alert box titled "We recommend following these guidelines when choosing your new password:" with 3 bullet points (at least 8 characters; uppercase/lowercase/special characters/numbers; avoid reusing old passwords).

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that submitting the reset password form without an email shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the Reset Password page.

steps.content: Enter a new password and confirm password, leaving the email field empty.
steps.expected: Password fields should be filled.

steps.content: Click "Reset Password".
steps.expected: "Email is required" error message should be displayed.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json) — no content change. Verified live via a real reset link provided out-of-band by the user (the same token, reused across the non-destructive validation cases in this section — it is not consumed until an actual successful reset completes).

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that submitting the reset password form with an invalid email format shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the Reset Password page.

steps.content: Enter an invalid email address (e.g. "notanemail", no "@") in the email field.
steps.expected: Invalid email should be entered.

steps.content: Enter a matching new and confirm password.
steps.expected: Password fields should be filled.

steps.content: Click "Reset Password".
steps.expected: The form does not submit (no request is sent to the reset API). Unlike the Forgot Password request page (whose email field is `type="text"`), this field is `type="email"` — the browser's own native email-format validation blocks it and shows its native tooltip (Chrome: "Please include an '@' in the email address. '<value>' is missing an '@'."), same as the Login page. The app itself displays no custom error message for a pure format error here.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json) — no content change. Verified live via the same real reset link.

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that submitting the reset password form with a mismatched email shows a token error
cases.preconditions: User is on the Reset Password page.

steps.content: Enter an email address that does not match the one used in the reset request.
steps.expected: Mismatched email should be entered.

steps.content: Enter a matching new and confirm password.
steps.expected: Password fields should be filled.

steps.content: Click "Reset Password".
steps.expected: "This password reset token is invalid." error message should be displayed inline below the confirm password field.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json) — no content change. Verified live via the same real reset link (a validly-registered but non-matching email). Note for future grilling: a token that is syntactically well-formed but never actually issued shows a different, full-page "This link is invalid" state instead — the inline message here is specific to a real, currently-valid token used with the wrong email.

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that submitting the reset password form without a new password shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the Reset Password page.

steps.content: Enter the registered email address and a confirm password, leaving the new password field empty.
steps.expected: Email and confirm password fields should be filled. Validation runs live (not just on submit) — "New Password is required" and "New Password and Confirm Password does not match" both appear inline as soon as the new password field is left empty while confirm password is filled.

steps.content: Click "Reset Password".
steps.expected: "New Password is required" error message should be displayed (the mismatch message from the previous step also remains displayed).

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json) — no content change. Verified live via the same real reset link — added the simultaneous mismatch message the original case didn't mention.

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that submitting the reset password form without a confirm password shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the Reset Password page.

steps.content: Enter the registered email address and a new password, leaving the confirm password field empty.
steps.expected: Email and new password fields should be filled.

steps.content: Click "Reset Password".
steps.expected: "Confirm password is required" error message should be displayed (lowercase "p" in "password" — draft previously said "Confirm Password").

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json) — no content change. Verified live via the same real reset link: expected: "Confirm Password is required" | actual: "Confirm password is required".

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that submitting the reset password form with a new password shorter than 8 characters shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the Reset Password page.

steps.content: Enter the registered email address and a matching confirm password.
steps.expected: Email and confirm password fields should be filled.

steps.content: Enter a new password with fewer than 8 characters.
steps.expected: Short password should be entered.

steps.content: Click "Reset Password".
steps.expected: "The password must be at least 8 characters long" error message should be displayed.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json) — no content change. Source expected result was marked [inferred] and was wrong: expected (inferred) "Minimum 8 characters required" | actual (verified live via the real reset link) "The password must be at least 8 characters long".

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that submitting the reset password form with a new password longer than 64 characters shows a validation error
cases.preconditions: User is on the Reset Password page.

steps.content: Enter the registered email address and a matching confirm password.
steps.expected: Email and confirm password fields should be filled.

steps.content: Enter a new password longer than 64 characters.
steps.expected: Oversized password should be entered — the field accepts arbitrary length client-side, with no `maxlength` truncation (confirmed a 68-character value is fully retained).

steps.content: Click "Reset Password".
steps.expected: "The password is too long" error message should be displayed.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json) — no content change. No run_type set in the original publish. Verified live via the same real reset link.

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that the reset password form accepts valid credentials and confirms the password reset
cases.preconditions: User is on the Reset Password page.

steps.content: Enter the registered email address.
steps.expected: Email should be entered.

steps.content: Enter a valid password (8–64 characters) in both the new password and confirm new password fields.
steps.expected: Matching passwords should be entered.

steps.content: Click "Reset Password".
steps.expected: The reset confirmation page should be displayed, showing a green checkmark icon, the heading "Your password is reset!", the message "You have successfully updated your password. Please log in with your new password.", and a "Log In" button. The request posts to `POST /api/v2/password/reset`.

steps.content: Verify that a "Your password has been changed" notification email is received at the registered address.
steps.expected: Password change notification email should be received.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json) — no content change. Step 3 (confirmation page) verified live via a real reset link the user provided out-of-band and completed for real — the free account's password was actually changed. Step 4 (email receipt) still not automatable — no inbox access configured.

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that the reset confirmation page renders with a success state and Log In button
cases.preconditions: User has successfully reset their password and is on the reset confirmation page.

steps.content: Verify the reset confirmation page is displayed with a success indicator.
steps.expected: A success confirmation page should be displayed with a green checkmark icon inside a green circle, the heading "Your password is reset!", and the message "You have successfully updated your password. Please log in with your new password."

steps.content: Verify the "Log In" button is present.
steps.expected: "Log In" button should be visible and interactive.

steps.content: Verify that a "Your password has been changed" notification email is received at the registered address.
steps.expected: Password change notification email should be received.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json) — no content change. First two steps verified live via a real completed reset. Third step (email receipt) still not automatable — no inbox access configured.

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that the "Log In" button on the reset confirmation page navigates to the login page
cases.preconditions: User has successfully reset their password and is on the reset confirmation page.

steps.content: Click "Log In".
steps.expected: Login page should be displayed.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json) — no content change. No run_type set in the original publish. Verified live end-to-end via a real completed reset: navigated to /login, then confirmed the new password (not the old one) actually logs in and lands on /dashboard.

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that clicking Reset Password with an expired token shows a token error
cases.preconditions: User initiated a password reset more than 1 hour ago and has the original "Password Reset Request" email in their inbox.

steps.content: Open the "Password Reset Request" email and click "Reset Password".
steps.expected: Reset Password page should be displayed.

steps.content: Enter the registered email address and matching new/confirm passwords.
steps.expected: All fields should be filled.

steps.content: Click "Reset Password".
steps.expected: "This password reset token is invalid." error message should be displayed below the confirm password field.

cases.rewrite_notes: Restored verbatim from prior publish (ai-context/cases-forgot-password.json) — no content change. Not automatable this pass: requires waiting 1+ hour on a real, previously-valid token (still needs a real email regardless). Adjacent finding from this grill run worth checking when this case is next run manually: a syntactically fake/never-issued token shows a different, full-page "This link is invalid" state (not this case's assumed inline message), while a real, currently-valid token used with the wrong email DOES show this exact inline message (verified — see the mismatched-email case). Whether a real token that has since expired behaves like the fake-token case or the wrong-email case is not yet known — worth confirming specifically when a real expired token is available.

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that submitting a forgot-password request for an unregistered email shows the same generic confirmation as a registered one
cases.preconditions: User is on the Forgot Password page. No account exists for the email address used in this test.

steps.content: Enter an email address with no registered account and click "Submit".
steps.expected: The same "Please check your inbox" confirmation page is displayed as for a registered email, quoting the submitted address back: "If there is an account associated with <email>, you will receive an email with a link to reset your password." The underlying `POST /api/v2/password/tokens` call also returns the same generic success response (`{"message":"We have e-mailed your password reset link!","status":"success"}`) regardless of whether the account exists — the app does not leak account existence through this flow.

cases.rewrite_notes: Found during gap-hunting — no existing draft case covers email-enumeration protection on the forgot-password request page.

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that a malformed or never-issued reset token shows a full-page invalid-link error
cases.preconditions: User navigates directly to a Reset Password URL with a token value that was never actually issued (e.g. `/reset/password?token=<random-string>`).

steps.content: Load the Reset Password page with the bogus token.
steps.expected: A full-page error state is shown instead of the reset form: a failed-icon graphic, the heading "This link is invalid", the message "Sorry, your password reset link may have expired or already been used. If you still want to reset your password, you can request a new reset link.", and a "Request a new password reset link" button. This differs from the in-form "This password reset token is invalid." message shown when a real, currently-valid token is used with a non-matching email — the two states are visually and structurally distinct.

cases.rewrite_notes: Found during gap-hunting — no existing draft case covers the full-page invalid-link state for a bogus/never-issued token; the draft's existing token-error cases (mismatched email, expired token) both assume the in-form inline message instead.

cases.title: [Forgot Password] Verify that repeated password-reset requests are rate-limited
cases.preconditions: User is on the Forgot Password page.

steps.content: Submit the forgot-password form repeatedly in quick succession.
steps.expected: The `POST /api/v2/password/tokens` endpoint enforces a rate limit — confirmed live via response headers (`X-RateLimit-Limit: 15`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining` decrementing with each call) — though the exact user-facing behavior once the limit is exhausted (error message, HTTP status, lockout duration) was not triggered during this pass to avoid exhausting the shared QA rate-limit budget. A dedicated pass should confirm the exceeded-limit behavior directly.

cases.rewrite_notes: Found during gap-hunting — rate-limit headers observed live but the exceeded-state itself deliberately not triggered this pass (would have consumed the shared IP's rate-limit budget for real reset requests). Flagged for a follow-up pass with a disposable IP/account rather than fully verified here.
